session_hint: the s3 presigned upload url expiring instantly
question: What did we conclude was causing S3 pre-signed URLs to expire immediately after generation?
turns: S3 pre-signed upload URLs generated by our EC2 instances are expiring almost immediately even though we configure a 15-minute (900 second) expiry. Clients receive 403 AccessDenied within seconds of getting the URL.
The X-Amz-Expires parameter in the URL correctly shows 900. But AWS validates that the signing time plus expiry is still in the future using real UTC. If the signing time is wrong, the window shifts.

Root cause is clock drift on the EC2 instance — the system clock was roughly 18 minutes behind real UTC. AWS computed signing_time + 900s and found it was already in the past by real-world time, so every URL was invalid before the client even received it. Fix: ensure NTP sync is running on the instance (chrony is the recommended daemon on Amazon Linux 2) and add a CloudWatch alarm on estimated_time_drift to catch future drift early.
